premise: An inscription mage is ordered to carve a death-mark into a condemned prisoner before dawn. When she makes the first cut, she finds his flesh is already covered in inscriptions she has never seen — a complete magical language predating the empire. The prisoner doesn't know they're there. He's had them since birth.

characters.name: Calla Vren
characters.description: Inscription mage, late 20s. Guild-trained, precise, performs executions as a contract obligation she's tried to stop thinking about. Reads inscriptions the way other people read faces — involuntarily, constantly. What she sees on the prisoner's skin doesn't match any known school or era.

characters.name: Davan
characters.description: The condemned prisoner. Former soldier, mid-30s, sentenced for a massacre he claims he didn't commit but stopped trying to argue. Calm in a way that reads as already-dead. When Calla shows him what's written on his body, he recognizes nothing — but his hands start shaking.

characters.name: Master Inquisitor Orvath
characters.description: The official who assigned Calla this execution. Forty years in the Guild. He knew about the inscriptions — he's why Davan was condemned. The execution was never about the massacre.
